# 'gs log short -S' shows 'blocked by #N' when an upstack branch's PR
# is still waiting on a downstack submitted PR to merge.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2025-09-23T19:12:00Z'

mkdir repo
cd repo
git init
git commit --allow-empty -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# set up a fake remote
shamhub init
shamhub register alice
shamhub new origin alice/example.git
git push origin main
env SHAMHUB_USERNAME=alice
gs auth login

# Build a 3-branch stack, submit all three.
git add feat1.txt
gs bc feat1 -m 'feat1'
git add feat2.txt
gs bc feat2 -m 'feat2'
git add feat3.txt
gs bc feat3 -m 'feat3'
gs dss --fill

# No PRs merged yet: the top two should be 'blocked by ...', and the
# bottom one should be 'ready' (nothing submitted-and-open below it).
gs ls -S
cmp stderr $WORK/golden/ls-all-open.txt

# Merge the bottom PR. Now feat2 is ready, and feat3 is still blocked
# by feat2 (the nearest unmerged submitted ancestor).
shamhub merge alice/example 1
gs ls -S
cmp stderr $WORK/golden/ls-feat1-merged.txt

-- repo/feat1.txt --
feat1
-- repo/feat2.txt --
feat2
-- repo/feat3.txt --
feat3

-- golden/ls-all-open.txt --
    ┏━■ feat3 (#3 open) blocked by #2 ◀
  ┏━┻□ feat2 (#2 open) blocked by #1
┏━┻□ feat1 (#1 open) ready
main
-- golden/ls-feat1-merged.txt --
    ┏━■ feat3 (#3 open) blocked by #2 ◀
  ┏━┻□ feat2 (#2 open) ready
┏━┻□ feat1 (#1 merged) merged
main
